---
title: "Algorithmic Trading: Winning Strategies and Their Rationale"
subtitle: "By Ernest P. Chan"
page-layout: article
---

# Purpose

This directory is a minimal Quarto stack for a quant lecture workflow:

- `slides/video-lesson.qmd` for video-first reveal.js slides
- `handouts/web-notes.qmd` for GitHub Pages style reading notes
- `handouts/pdf-notes.qmd` for printable notes

The goal is one content stack with different presentation styles:

- video output favors pacing, fragments, notes, and autoplay
- web/PDF output favors completeness, readability, and review

# Why This Stack

It covers the constraints that matter for a quant course:

- LaTeX-style math without writing full Beamer
- executable Python code blocks when you want them
- chart-heavy content: PnL, drawdown, histograms, rolling metrics
- code snippets with syntax highlighting
- speaker notes that can double as a TTS script source
- web publishing and PDF export from the same project

# Files

- [Video Slides](slides/video-lesson.qmd)
- [Web Notes](handouts/web-notes.qmd)
- [PDF Notes](handouts/pdf-notes.qmd)
- [Project README](README.md)

# Suggested Workflow

1. Write the main teaching flow in `slides/video-lesson.qmd`.
2. Keep speaker script inside slide notes blocks.
3. Move derivations, caveats, and extra charts into the handouts.
4. Generate TTS audio from notes and align durations to slide `auto-slide`.
5. Publish `_site/` to GitHub Pages and export PDF for offline reading.
